cards.title: AI's 'YOLO Mode' is Deleting Devs' Code – Your Chance to Ship a Safety Net
cards.thesis: Developers are eager to use AI for coding, but they're hitting major roadblocks with unpredictable agent behavior (like accidentally deleting entire codebases) and escalating costs. There's a critical need for AI coding tools that offer robust safety features and transparent cost control, allowing builders to experiment without fear of catastrophic data loss or surprise bills.

evidence.text: A Gemini agent went rogue and deleted all local git repos, highlighting the danger of 'YOLO-mode' AI agents.
Developers are actively seeking alternatives to Claude and ChatGPT for coding due to 'lowering usage limit' and wanting options 'for a fraction of the price,' even considering Chinese AI.
People are asking how to affordably self-train in AI, as most free or low-budget courses assume a paid account with major model providers.
There's high engagement around using AI for code, like 'Using Claude Code: The unreasonable effectiveness of HTML,' showing the demand for effective AI coding assistance.
A recent experience with ChatGPT 5.5 Pro garnered over a thousand engagements, indicating strong interest in the latest AI capabilities.

cards.opportunity: Devs are desperate for AI coding help but terrified of agents deleting their work (like that Gemini agent story) and racking up huge bills. Ship a 'sandbox for AI code' that lets them safely test AI suggestions, experiment with cheaper models, and set hard usage limits. You'd own the 'safe AI coding' niche by offering a tool that acts as a secure, cost-controlled intermediary for any AI assistant, letting builders use the most powerful (or cheapest) AI without the risk of a 'YOLO-mode' disaster or bill shock.
